column3: NAFTA, or the North American Free Trade Agreement, was created 20 years ago to expand trade between the U.S., Canada and Mexico. It's secondary purpose was to make these countries more competitive in the global marketplace. It has been wildly successful in achieving this. NAFTA is now the largest free trade agreement in the world . One of the problems with NAFTA is that it's reduced U.S. jobs. A second disadvantage is that it has exploited Mexico's farmers and its environment .Rural Mexican farmers could not compete with low-cost corn and other grains that were exported by subsidized U.S. farm corporations. NAFTA put more than a million Mexican farmers were out of business. The ones that remained were forced to use more fertilizers and farm marginal land, resulting in more pollution and deforestation. Labor was cheap in Mexico because they had no labor rights or health protection. Thanks to NAFTA, nearly a third of Mexico's labor force were in this "maquiladora" program. I live in The City of Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico. I grew up with this program and I find this so unfair. NAFTA called for unlimited transportation between the three member countries. This would have created a dangerous situation, since Mexican trucks were not up to U.%%S. safety standards, and Mexican roads could not have carried the heavier U.S. trucks. This problem was averted in 2008, when Congress prohibited this portion of NAFTA from ever being carried out
column6: Writing a brochure for a museum

column3: The last year United States and Colombia made an agreement in order to reduce tariffs in import and export products. That is what we called "free trade". This agreement caused a big manifestation in Colombia due to the fact, merchants and entrepreneurs will going to lose privileges and their products will no longer be the choice for the buyers. According to the U.S Colombias economy is the third largest in Central and South America. It will create a new market for the entrepreneurs and traders and also is going to expand/promote economic for both. In addition, this kind of negotiations help to show more about our home products, culture and folklore. However, Many people in my country don't agree with it. Personally, I have to say that having the possibility to expand our market to new places is very positive for everyone. For example, people who travel overseas will be able to find goods from their native country. On the other hand, if that is not possible, they shall be obliged to consume food and goods that maybe they don't want to try. Moreover, giving the opportunity to dealers from outside show their products, will open our mind and perspective about the world and what they have to offer us.%%
column6: Writing a brochure for a museum
